Vendored runtime dependencies.

Pinned versions:
  prompt_toolkit==3.0.52   -- interactive picker (interactive_picker.py)
  wcwidth==0.6.0           -- prompt_toolkit's only runtime dependency
  mistune==3.1.4           -- Markdown -> HTML rendering (view_conversation.py)
  pygments==2.19.2         -- code-block syntax highlighting (view_conversation.py)

Why vendored:
  The interactive search-result picker is the default UX, so its dependency
  must be present without a `pip install` step. prompt_toolkit's 3.x line is
  stable and the surface we use (Application, Window, FormattedTextControl,
  KeyBindings) has been unchanged for years. wcwidth is prompt_toolkit's only
  runtime dependency.

  `view_conversation.py --format html` renders message Markdown to HTML so it
  reads nicely in a browser. mistune is a pure-Python Markdown parser; pygments
  highlights fenced code blocks. Both are pure Python with no dependencies.

To refresh:
  1. pip download --no-deps 'prompt_toolkit==<new>' 'wcwidth==<new>' \
       'mistune==<new>' 'pygments==<new>' -d /tmp/dl
  2. rm -rf vendor/prompt_toolkit vendor/wcwidth vendor/mistune vendor/pygments
  3. cd vendor && python3 -m zipfile -e /tmp/dl/prompt_toolkit-*.whl .
                  python3 -m zipfile -e /tmp/dl/wcwidth-*.whl .
                  python3 -m zipfile -e /tmp/dl/mistune-*.whl .
                  python3 -m zipfile -e /tmp/dl/pygments-*.whl .
     (Keep the .dist-info directories — prompt_toolkit reads its own version
     via importlib.metadata at import time and will fail without them.)
  4. Update the pinned versions above.

Loaded via vendor_loader.py, which prepends `vendor/` to sys.path.
